guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"336f2","text":"INTRODUCTION","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1p6f9","text":"The SpinOUTâ„˘ columns are versatile, spin-format columns for the desalting and buffer exchange of peptide and protein and other macromolecule solutions ranging from 5Âµl through to 3ml sample volumes.Â The SpinOUTâ„˘ columns are available in three MWCO sizes for \u003e700, \u003e6,000 or \u003e30,000 dalton peptides or proteins and are suitable for samples containing as little as 20Âµg peptide or protein/ml.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"91m2p","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6d4g7","text":"The SpinOUTâ„˘ columns are simple to use as the peptide or protein solution is applied and then centrifuged to recover protein with the column retaining \u003e95% of the salts and small moleculesÂ (\u003c100Da for SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-100, \u003c1,000Da for Spinoutâ„˘ GT-600 andÂ \u003c1,500 for Spinoutâ„˘ GT-1200).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7qmpu","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bflj0","text":"ITEMS SUPPLIED","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dlc7e","text":"   Cat. # steps: Equilibrate the column with the buffer of choice by applying at least 5 column volumes of buffer in batches of 1-2 column volumes.
Centrifuge the column at 1,000g for 2 minutes to remove the buffer after each application. 00:02:00
Place the column in a new collection tube and remove the cap.

Slowly, apply the protein solution to the center of the SpinOUTâ„˘ resin. The following are the recommended volumes to apply to the column. %s %s Cat. # Description # Supplied Resin Bed Volume (ml) Sample Load Volume (ml) 786-865 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-100, 0.1ml 25 0.1 0.005-0.03 786-866 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-100, 1ml 10 1 0.03-0.3 786-867 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-100, 3ml 10 3 0.3-0.9 786-868 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-100, 5ml 5 5 0.75-1.5 786-869 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-100, 10ml 5 10 1-3 786-703 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-600, 0.1ml 25 0.1 0.005-0.03 786-170 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-600, 1ml 10 1 0.03-0.3 786-171 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-600, 3ml 10 3 0.3-0.9 786-704 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-600, 5ml 5 5 0.75-1.5 786-705 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-600, 10ml 5 10 1-3 786-706 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-1200, 0.1ml 25 0.1 0.005-0.03 786-172 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-1200, 1ml 10 1 0.03-0.3 786-173 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-1200, 3ml 10 3 0.3-0.9 786-707 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-1200, 5ml 5 5 0.75-1.5 786-708 SpinOUTâ„˘ GT-1200, 10ml 5 10 1-3 Sample Load Volume The recommended load volumes (see table) are a guideline. The actual volumes used will be dependent on your sample, the concentration of salts and contaminants to beremoved and the recovered purity desired. For optimal removal of contaminants, we recommend using a sample volume of <20% of the resin bed volume. NOTE : Loading more than the recommended load volume will result in a higher level of contaminating salts and other molecules.

Centrifuge the column at 1,000g for the indicated times in the table below to collect the desalted protein solution. Discard the column. %s %s Column size Centrifugation Time (mins) 0.1ml column 4 1ml column 4 3ml column 6 5ml column 8 10ml column 10 Table 1: Centrifugation times for optimal sample recovery.
Mark one side of the column and ensure in all centrifugations the mark is facing outwards during centrifugation.
Prepare the SpinOUTâ„˘ column by centrifuging the SpinOUTâ„˘ columns at 1,000g for 1 minute to compact the resin. 00:01:00
Remove the top and then bottom caps. Place into an appropriate collection tube.
Centrifuge the column at 1,000g for 2 minutes to remove the storage buffer. 00:02:00
